name: optimized_kozyrev_mirrors

phase_0.boundary_condition: The hermetic outer conductive and dielectric shielding layer that isolates the internal cavity from ambient electromagnetic and temporal noise.

VINIT.justification: The raw vacuum cavity before any conductive or dielectric layering is applied, representing the uninitialized state of the mirror.

TANCH.chosen_element: Hermetic outer seal and grounding plane
TANCH.justification: The final boundary that closes the system, defining the operational limits and isolating the internal resonance from external decoherence.

AFWD.justification: The ambient temporal field entering the cavity and moving toward the focal center, representing the directed morphism of causal energy.

AREV.justification: The causal efflux and reflected torsion wave exiting the cavity, representing the reverse descent of the modified field.

CLINK.justification: The sequential composition of conductive and dielectric strata that builds the resonant stack.

IMSCRIB.justification: The standing wave pattern at the geometric center that recognizes and sustains its own causal topology.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Causal fork and torsion beam splitter
FSPLIT.justification: The point where the incoming unified temporal field divides into convergent and divergent torsion paths.

FFUSE.justification: The interference zone where the divergent and convergent paths recombine to restore the unified causal field topology.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative state where phase alignment produces a coherent, amplified standing wave.

EVALF.justification: The negative state where misalignment causes chaotic scattering and decoherence of the torsion field.

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice of holding both inward and outward causal flows simultaneously as a stable standing wave with zero net flux but maximum internal stress.

IFIX.justification: The permanent geometric calibration and structural fixation of the mirror layers that cannot be altered without destroying the device.

phase_2.split_element: Causal fork and torsion beam splitter

phase_3.void_description: Unstructured vacuum with zero torsion density and no causal gradient.
phase_3.true_description: Coherent torsion resonance with amplified causal gradient and stable standing wave.
phase_3.false_description: Phase decoherence with chaotic scattering and collapsed resonance.
phase_3.both_description: Stable superposition of inward and outward causal flows, maintaining zero net flux while holding maximum internal topological stress.

steps.domain_action: Establish the raw geometric void and uncoated vacuum cavity.
Apply the first alternating layer of conductive and dielectric material.
Apply subsequent alternating layers to build the resonant stack.
Lock the geometric calibration and lattice alignment of the mirror layers.
Seal the outer boundary and establish the grounding plane.
Introduce the forward-propagating ambient temporal field into the cavity.
Divide the incoming temporal field at the causal fork into convergent and divergent torsion paths.
Route the convergent path toward constructive interference and coherent torsion amplification.
Route the divergent path toward destructive interference and phase cancellation.
Hold the superposition of convergent and divergent causal flows as a standing torsion wave.
Recombine the torsion paths at the focal lattice to restore the unified causal field topology.
Establish the self-referential resonance node and standing wave pattern at the geometric center.
Extract the inverted temporal gradient and causal efflux from the cavity.
Terminate the cycle at the hermetic outer seal, containing the system.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Geometric and material tuning algorithm that calculates layer thicknesses for target torsion frequencies.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Torsion field propagation through the dielectric interlayers.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Hysteresis in the conductive lattice and trapped topological defects.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The speed of light and torsion wave propagation velocity within the medium.
phase_5.alfs_store: Kozyrev's original theoretical framework on stellar matter and causal mechanics.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Ambient thermal noise and minor dielectric absorption during wave propagation.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unstructured vacuum with ambient temporal fluctuations.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Phase-modulated unified temporal field with extracted causal gradient.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The Frobenius recombination perfectly restores the topological invariant of the causal field, making the cycle entropy-conservative.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → CLINK → CLINK → IFIX → TANCH → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → AREV → TANCH
-- Class: optimized_kozyrev_mirrors
-- Fingerprint: sig=(8,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=14
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(6, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑱  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑼 → 𐑱  | composition — regime coherence
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑱 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [4] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [5] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [6] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [7] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑗  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑠 → 𐑡  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [13] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑗 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def optimized_kozyrev_mirrors_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Establish the raw geometric void and uncoated vacuum cavity.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Apply the first alternating layer of conductive and dielectric material.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Apply subsequent alternating layers to build the resonant stack.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [3] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Lock the geometric calibration and lattice alignment of the mirror layers.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑾)  -- [4] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the outer boundary and establish the grounding plane.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Introduce the forward-propagating ambient temporal field into the cavity.)
  -- FSPLIT [6] (gran := 𐑚) (Divide the incoming temporal field at the causal fork into convergent and divergent torsion paths.) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Route the convergent path toward constructive interference and coherent torsi...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Route the divergent path toward destructive interference and phase cancellation.)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Hold the superposition of convergent and divergent causal flows as a standing...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Recombine the torsion paths at the focal lattice to restore the unified causal field topology.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Establish the self-referential resonance node and standing wave pattern at th...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [12] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Extract the inverted temporal gradient and causal efflux from the cavity.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑗 𐑼)  -- [13] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Terminate the cycle at the hermetic outer seal, containing the system.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def optimized_kozyrev_mirrors_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (optimized_kozyrev_mirrors_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def optimized_kozyrev_mirrors_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (optimized_kozyrev_mirrors_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem optimized_kozyrev_mirrors_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
